name: Nandi Hills
teaser: Misty sunrise views and ancient hilltop temples just 60 km from Bangalore
about: Famous for spectacular early morning mist and Tipu Sultan's summer residence. Nandi Fort sits at the summit with panoramic views of the Deccan plateau.

name: Coorg (Madikeri)
teaser: Scotland of India — coffee estates, misty peaks and the Abbey Falls
about: Coorg is Karnataka's most popular hill destination. Coffee and spice plantations carpet the hills, punctuated by waterfalls, tribal villages and the ancient Madikeri Fort.

name: Chikmagalur
teaser: Birthplace of coffee in India, with misty peaks and trekking trails
about: Chikmagalur sits in the Baba Budan Giri range. Home to Mullayanagiri — Karnataka's highest peak — and vast coffee estates. Ideal for trekking and nature walks.

name: Sakleshpur
teaser: Misty coffee hills and the scenic Bisle Forest trek gateway
about: A quieter alternative to Coorg. Sakleshpur is surrounded by coffee and cardamom estates, with the famous Bisle Ghat viewpoint and heritage railway trail nearby.

name: Kemmangundi
teaser: A hidden hill garden with rose parks, rhododendrons and valley viewpoints
about: Kemmangundi was Mysore's royal retreat. Known for its botanical gardens, Shanti Falls and the Raj Bhavan bungalow. Z-point offers sweeping Western Ghats views.

name: Kudremukh
teaser: Horse-face peak over rolling grasslands and shola forests in the Ghats
about: Kudremukh (Horse Face) is a UNESCO World Heritage site and one of Karnataka's most scenic treks. The route passes through dense shola forest, grasslands and streams.

name: Kodachadri
teaser: Trek through shola forests to a hilltop Mookambika shrine and sea views
about: Kodachadri sits at 1,343 m and is home to the Sarvajna Peetha — a sacred shrine. The trek offers stunning views of the Arabian Sea on clear days and dense Ghats forest.

name: Kumara Parvata
teaser: Karnataka's toughest peak trek — 13 km of steep ghats forest
about: At 1,712 m, Kumara Parvata (Pushpagiri) is one of Karnataka's most demanding day treks. The trail from Kukke Subramanya passes through thick jungle before opening to grassland meadows.

name: Tadiandamol Trek
teaser: Coorg's highest peak through misty shola forest and open meadows
about: Tadiandamol at 1,748 m is Coorg's tallest summit. The moderate trail starts from Kakkabe village and climbs through shola forests to breezy grasslands with 360° views.

name: Ettina Bhuja Trek
teaser: Ox-shoulder ridge trek with dramatic cliff-edge views over Chikmagalur valley
about: Ettina Bhuja (Ox's Shoulder) is a striking ridge walk with sheer drops on either side. Short but steep, it rewards trekkers with some of the most dramatic Western Ghats panoramas.

name: Devarayanadurga
teaser: Granite boulder fortress with twin peaks and ancient temples near Tumkur
about: Devarayanadurga is a rocky hill fortress with two peaks — Narasimha Swamy Betta and Bhoga Narasimha Betta. The rocky terrain makes it great for a quick day climb from Bangalore.

name: Baba Budangiri
teaser: Misty sacred peaks where a Sufi saint brought coffee seeds to India
about: Baba Budan Giri is revered by both Hindus and Muslims. The dargah of Sufi saint Dada Hayat Mir Khalander sits at the summit — he is said to have smuggled coffee seeds from Yemen here in the 17th century.

name: Mandalpatti
teaser: A secret plateau above the clouds reachable only by jeep safari from Madikeri
about: Mandalpatti is a high-altitude meadow plateau accessible only by forest jeep. At 1,500 m, it rises above the morning clouds to reveal sweeping Coorg valley views.

name: Kundadri Hills
teaser: Jain hilltop temple and panoramic Ghats views on an off-the-radar peak
about: Kundadri is a forested hill with a Jain temple at the summit. The trek passes through dense forest and ends at open rocky plateau with views of the Agumbe reserve forest below.

name: Agumbe
teaser: The Cherrapunji of South India — king cobra territory and monsoon waterfalls
about: Agumbe receives the second highest rainfall in India. It's famous for king cobra research (ARRS), dense rainforest, sunset point and beautiful waterfalls that peak in the monsoon.

name: Nagarhole National Park
teaser: Tigers, elephants and leopards in Karnataka's finest jungle reserve
about: Nagarhole (Rajiv Gandhi National Park) is part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve. It boasts high densities of tiger, leopard, wild elephant, gaur and more. Kabini River runs through its heart.

name: Bandipur National Park
teaser: One of India's best tiger reserves on the Mysore–Ooty highway
about: Bandipur Tiger Reserve spans 912 sq km and is part of the Nilgiri Biosphere. Dry deciduous forests host tiger, elephant, sloth bear and hundreds of bird species.

name: Bhadra Wildlife Sanctuary
teaser: Boat safari through a tiger reserve hidden in the Chikmagalur hills
about: Bhadra Tiger Reserve is one of Karnataka's least-crowded wildlife destinations. Boat safaris on the Bhadra reservoir offer sightings of sloth bear, leopard, tiger and large herds of gaur.

name: Daroji Bear Sanctuary
teaser: India's only dedicated sloth bear sanctuary next to Hampi's boulder landscape
about: Daroji is Asia's largest sloth bear sanctuary. The rocky, scrub-forested terrain near Hampi hosts over 120 sloth bears. An elevated viewing platform gives safe, close sightings.

name: Hampi
teaser: Ruins of a 14th-century empire scattered across a surreal boulder landscape
about: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, once one of the world's largest cities. Over 1,600 monuments — temples, market streets, palaces and water tanks — dot a landscape of giant granite boulders and the Tungabhadra river.

name: Badami
teaser: 6th-century Chalukya cave temples carved into red sandstone cliffs
about: Badami's four rock-cut cave temples (3 Hindu, 1 Jain) are among India's finest early temple architecture. Carved into a red sandstone gorge above a sacred lake, they date to 578 AD.

name: Aihole
teaser: The cradle of Indian temple architecture — 125 temples in a single village
about: Called the 'Cradle of Indian Architecture', Aihole has over 125 temple structures spanning 1,500 years of experiment. The Durga Temple is one of India's earliest examples of the apsidal plan.

name: Pattadakal
teaser: UNESCO Chalukya temple complex where North and South Indian styles first met
about: Pattadakal is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with 10 major Chalukya temples from the 7th–8th century. It represents the earliest known synthesis of North Indian (Nagara) and South Indian (Dravida) temple architecture.

name: Gol Gumbaz
teaser: India's second largest dome and the whispering gallery of Mohammed Adil Shah
about: Gol Gumbaz is the mausoleum of Mohammed Adil Shah, built in 1656. Its 38 m diameter dome is the second largest in the world. The circular gallery at its base is famous for acoustic whispering effects.

name: Bidar Fort
teaser: 15th-century Bahmani capital with Persian-tiled palaces and hidden hammams
about: Bidar Fort is the largest medieval fort in Karnataka, built by the Bahmani Sultanate in 1428. Inside are the Rangin Mahal with coloured tile work, a Turkish bath and a 1.5 km deep moat.

name: Gulbarga Fort
teaser: First Bahmani Sultanate capital with a unique indoor mosque and royal tombs
about: Gulbarga Fort houses a unique mosque modeled on the mosque at Córdoba, Spain — the only fully covered mosque in India. The fort was the first capital of the Bahmani Kingdom (1347).

name: Kittur Fort
teaser: Where Rani Chennamma fought the British East India Company in 1824
about: Kittur Fort is historically significant as the seat of Rani Chennamma, who led the first armed resistance against British rule in Karnataka in 1824 — 33 years before the 1857 uprising.

name: Talakad
teaser: A sand-buried ancient city of 30 Shiva temples on the banks of the Kaveri
about: Talakad is a mysterious ancient town on the Kaveri river where shifting sand dunes have buried most of its 30+ Shiva temples over centuries. Excavations still reveal new structures. Holds deep religious significance for the Panchalinga darshan.

name: Somanathapura Temple
teaser: A perfect star-shaped Hoysala masterpiece with 200 intricate friezes
about: The Keshava Temple at Somanathapura is one of the finest and most complete Hoysala temples. Built in 1268 AD, its triple-shrined star-shaped plan is covered with six horizontal bands of exquisitely detailed sculpture.

name: Belur (Chennakeshava Temple)
teaser: Hoysala masterwork — 103 years in the making, 648 elephants at the base
about: The Chennakeshava Temple at Belur took 103 years to build under three Hoysala kings. Its outer walls feature 648 elephants, interspersed with warriors, scenes of daily life and the famous bracket figures of celestial women.

name: Shravanabelagola
teaser: The 57-foot monolithic Gomateshvara — tallest freestanding statue in the world
about: Shravanabelagola is one of the most important Jain pilgrimage sites. The 17.4 m (57 ft) monolithic statue of Gomateshvara Bahubali stands atop Vindhyagiri Hill and dates to 983 AD.

name: Melukote
teaser: Hilltop Vaishnava town where Ramanuja once lived and composed treatises
about: Melukote (Thirunarayanapuram) is one of Karnataka's most revered Vaishnava pilgrimage centres. The Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple atop the hill was revived by philosopher-saint Ramanuja in the 12th century.

name: Nanjangud (Srikanteshvara Temple)
teaser: One of South India's most important Shiva temples on the Kabini river
about: The Srikanteshvara Temple at Nanjangud is a prominent Shaiva pilgrimage site. Built over several centuries, the temple's towering gopuram and sacred Kabini river ghat draw thousands of devotees. Known as 'Dakshina Kashi'.

name: Kukke Subramanya
teaser: Sacred snake temple in a valley surrounded by the Pushpagiri hills
about: Kukke Subramanya temple is dedicated to Lord Subramanya — protector of snakes. One of the richest temples in Karnataka, it sits in a lush forest valley at the base of Kumara Parvata. Famous for the Sarpa Dosha ritual.

name: Dharmasthala
teaser: A unique pilgrimage town serving free meals to 10,000 devotees daily
about: Dharmasthala is a famous Shaiva pilgrimage centre managed by a Jain family — a unique model of religious harmony. The temple serves free meals (annasantarpane) to over 10,000 visitors daily. The 39-foot Bahubali statue nearby is also notable.

name: Kollur Mookambika Temple
teaser: Sacred Devi temple in a forest valley at the foot of Kodachadri
about: Mookambika Temple at Kollur is one of the seven sacred Mukti Kshetras in Kerala and Karnataka. The goddess Mookambika is worshipped as a combined form of Parvati, Lakshmi and Saraswati. Set in a lush forest valley.

name: Hornadu Annapoorneshwari Temple
teaser: A forest-island temple of the goddess of food, with free meals for all
about: The Annapoorneshwari Temple at Hornadu sits on a small island formed by the confluence of three rivers. The goddess Annapoorneshwari blesses devotees with food. The temple provides free meals to all visitors without discrimination.

name: Sringeri Sharada Peetham
teaser: Adi Shankaracharya's first and most sacred monastery by the Tunga river
about: Sringeri Sharada Peetham is the first of the four mathas established by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century. Set on the banks of the Tunga river, it houses the Sharadamba temple and is one of Hinduism's most revered seats of learning.

name: Udupi Sri Krishna Temple
teaser: 800-year-old Krishna temple famed for the window-of-nine-holes darshan
about: The Sri Krishna Temple at Udupi was established by Madhvacharya in the 13th century. The deity is viewed through a window of nine holes (Kanakana Kindi) — named after the saint Kanakadasa. The temple's unique traditions and Paryaya festival attract pilgrims year-round.

name: ISKCON Temple Bangalore
teaser: One of ISKCON's largest temples — an island of calm in the heart of Bangalore
about: The ISKCON temple in Rajajinagar is one of the largest ISKCON temples in the world. A peaceful retreat within Bangalore, with beautiful marble carvings, cultural programming and daily arati ceremonies.

name: Kotilingeshwara Temple
teaser: 108-foot Shivalinga surrounded by a crore (10 million) Shivalingas
about: Kotilingeshwara is a unique temple housing a 108-foot Shivalinga and over one crore (10 million) smaller Shivalingas covering 15 acres. Devotees donate a Shivalinga when prayers are fulfilled.

name: Ramanagara (Sholay Hills)
teaser: Iconic silk city boulders — Bollywood's Sholay location and a rock climber's playground
about: Ramanagara is famous for its dramatic granite boulders that were used in the filming of Bollywood's Sholay. Today it's Karnataka's best rock climbing destination, with bolted routes for all levels. Also the world's largest silk market.

name: Shivanasamudra Falls
best_time: Jul – Oct (peak), Nov – Feb (calm for coracles)
teaser: India's first hydroelectric falls — twin cascades of the Kaveri over the Deccan edge
about: Shivanasamudra is a segmented waterfall where the Kaveri river splits around an island and plunges 90 m in two streams — Gaganachukki and Bharachukki. Site of India's first hydroelectric plant (1902).

name: Jog Falls
teaser: India's second highest waterfall — four streams plunging 253 m into a misty gorge
about: Jog Falls is formed by the Sharavathi river plunging 253 m in four distinct streams — Raja, Rani, Rocket and Roarer. Best viewed during and immediately after monsoon when flow is maximum.

name: Abbey Falls
teaser: A 70-foot cascade surrounded by coffee estate canopy in the heart of Coorg
about: Abbey Falls (Abbi Falls) is Coorg's most visited waterfall. The 70-foot drop flows through privately owned coffee and spice estates. A hanging bridge over the gorge gives the best viewing angle.

name: Sirimane Falls
teaser: A hidden three-tiered waterfall deep in the Agumbe forest — swimming allowed
about: Sirimane Falls is a three-tiered waterfall on the Sauparnika river. Unlike most waterfalls, swimming is permitted in the lower pool. Accessible via a 2 km forest trail from the ticket counter.

name: Magod Falls
teaser: Twin falls plunging into a dramatic gorge in the dense Uttara Kannada forest
about: Magod Falls on the Bedti river drops 200 m in two stages into a deep forest gorge. The viewpoint offers dramatic visuals across the Western Ghats canopy. Largely off the tourist radar.

name: Sathodi Falls
teaser: Karnataka's Niagara — a wide curtain fall over moss-covered boulders
about: Sathodi Falls (also called Mini Niagara) is a wide cascade on the Kali tributary. The falls fan out over mossy black rocks into a broad pool. Accessible via a 3 km trek through reserve forest.

name: Mallalli Falls
teaser: A powerful 200-foot single drop in the Coorg jungle — surprisingly uncrowded
about: Mallalli Falls on the Kumaradhara river is one of Coorg's tallest and least-visited waterfalls at 60 m. A 2 km trek through bamboo forest leads to the base. The volume of water during monsoon is spectacular.

name: Gokarna
teaser: Sacred beach town with cliff-hop trails between secluded coves
about: Gokarna is a sacred Hindu town with one of the most beautiful beach trails in India. The Om Beach (shaped like the om symbol), Half Moon Beach and Paradise Beach are reachable by cliff trail or boat — mostly free of commercial development.

details.mahabaleshwar_temple: One of India's most sacred Shiva temples

name: Om Beach
teaser: The beach shaped like the Om symbol — Gokarna's iconic curved cove
about: Om Beach is Gokarna's most photographed beach, named for its natural Om shape when viewed from above. The twin crescent bays are separated by a rocky headland. Relatively low commercialisation compared to Goa.

name: Kudle Beach
teaser: A long calm crescent beach with beach shacks and a traveller village vibe
about: Kudle is Gokarna's longest beach and most developed for tourism — though still very relaxed compared to Goa. A row of shacks lines the beach, and the hillside has basic guesthouses with sea views.

name: Karwar Beach
teaser: Rabindranath Tagore's inspiration — a calm bay at the mouth of the Kali river
about: Karwar is a calm, non-touristy coastal town where Rabindranath Tagore stayed in 1882 and wrote poetry inspired by the beauty of the bay. The INS Chapal Warship Museum is also here.

details.naval_base: Adjoins INS Kadamba (largest naval base in Asia)

name: Malpe Beach
teaser: Boat to St. Mary's Island — Vasco da Gama's landing point with hexagonal basalt columns
about: Malpe is Udupi's main beach and the gateway to St. Mary's Island. The island features stunning hexagonal basalt rock formations — similar to the Giant's Causeway — and is where Vasco da Gama first landed in India in 1498.

details.water_sports: Jet ski, banana boat, speed boat

name: St. Mary's Island
teaser: An island of ancient basalt hexagons — geology and history in the Arabian Sea
about: St. Mary's Island is a protected geological monument featuring 88-million-year-old hexagonal basalt columns formed by volcanic activity when India separated from Africa. Vasco da Gama erected a cross here in 1498.

name: Panambur Beach (Mangalore)
teaser: Mangalore's wide golden beach with kite-flying festivals and water sports
about: Panambur Beach is one of Mangalore's best beaches, known for annual sand art and kite festivals. Water sports options include jet ski, parasailing and banana boat. The port area has interesting backwater scenery.

name: Murdeshwar
teaser: World's second-tallest Shiva statue rising 123 feet over the Arabian Sea
about: Murdeshwar is a coastal temple town dominated by the iconic 123-foot Shiva statue. The Mrideshwara Temple sits on a rocky promontory into the sea. A gopuram offers lift access to the top for sweeping coastal views.

details.beach: Sandy beach below the temple complex

name: Devbagh Beach
teaser: A secluded island beach where the Kali river meets the Arabian Sea
about: Devbagh is a peaceful beach island accessible by boat from Karwar. It's where the Kali river meets the sea, creating clear blue-green water on the river side and the open Arabian Sea on the other. Known for dolphin sightings.

name: Maravante Beach
teaser: A beach pinched between the sea and the Souparnika river — sunrise and sunset from one spot
about: Maravante is a narrow strip of land with the Arabian Sea on one side and the Souparnika River estuary on the other. You can watch the sunrise over the river and the sunset over the sea from virtually the same spot.

details.unique_feature: Sea on one side, river estuary on the other

name: Someshwara Beach
teaser: Rocky shore, ancient Shiva temple and the best sunset in Mangalore
about: Someshwara Beach south of Mangalore has a dramatic rocky coastline with tidal pools and a small but atmospheric Shiva temple at the shore. Much less crowded than Panambur.

name: Ullal Beach
teaser: A wide, quiet beach next to the tomb of Sayyed Mohammed Shareef — Tipu Sultan's favourite
about: Ullal is a peaceful beach town south of Mangalore. Sultan Rani Abbakka, India's first woman freedom fighter who resisted the Portuguese, is from Ullal. The Dargah of Sayed Mohammed Shareef is a significant pilgrimage spot.

name: Half Moon Beach
teaser: No road, no noise — only reachable by a 30-min cliff trail or boat from Om Beach
about: Half Moon Beach is a secluded crescent cove accessible only by a 30-minute cliff trail from Om Beach or by boat. No motorized vehicles, minimal mobile signal. Basic campsites along the shore.

details.access: Cliff trail (30 min) or boat from Om Beach

name: Mysore Palace
teaser: India's most visited monument after the Taj — lit by 97,000 bulbs on Sundays
about: The Mysore Palace (Amba Vilas Palace) is the official residence of the Wadiyar dynasty. Built in 1912 by Henry Irwin in Indo-Saracenic style, it is India's most visited palace. The Sunday illumination of 97,000 bulbs is iconic.

details.illumination: 97,000 bulbs every Sunday, public holidays and Dasara

name: Biligirirangana Hills (BR Hills)
teaser: The only place in India where Eastern and Western Ghats meet — elephants and Soliga tribe
about: BR Hills is a unique biodiversity hotspot where the Eastern and Western Ghats converge, creating unusually high wildlife density. Inhabited by the Soliga tribe, it has an elephant population of over 2,500 and is a Tiger Reserve.

name: Kabini River Camps
best_time: Mar – Jun (black panther sightings), Oct – Feb (general)
teaser: Boat safaris at dusk where elephants wade across the Kabini river
about: Kabini is the southern zone of Nagarhole Tiger Reserve. The reservoir backwaters attract hundreds of animals at dusk — the boat safari here is among India's finest wildlife experiences. Kabini also has a famous black panther resident.

details.key_experience: Evening boat safari on Kabini backwaters

name: Manjarabad Fort
teaser: Tipu Sultan's star-shaped fort perched above the coffee mist of Sakleshpur
about: Manjarabad Fort is a well-preserved 18th-century star-shaped fort built by Tipu Sultan. Set at 962 m above sea level in the Western Ghats, it offers commanding views over the Sakleshpur coffee belt and passes.

name: Devanahalli Fort & Tipu's Birthplace
teaser: The birthplace of Tipu Sultan — a crumbling fort just past Bangalore airport
about: Devanahalli Fort is where Tipu Sultan was born in 1750. The fort dates to 1501 and the Venkataramana Swamy temple inside is active. The birthplace monument and museum are within the fort complex.

name: Shettyhalli Church (Ruins)
teaser: A 19th-century French church that rises ghost-like from the Hemavathi reservoir
about: The Shettyhalli Rosary Church was built by French missionaries in the 1860s. When the Hemavathi dam was built in 1960, the village and church were submerged. In summer when water levels drop, the Gothic ruins re-emerge dramatically from the lake — a photographer's favourite.

details.visibility: Best Aug–Oct (partially submerged), fully visible Jan–Apr

name: Ranganathittu Bird Sanctuary
teaser: Karnataka's largest bird sanctuary — 200 species, crocodiles and boat rides on the Kaveri
about: Ranganathittu on the Kaveri river is Karnataka's largest bird sanctuary. Six rocky islets host breeding colonies of painted storks, open-billed storks, spoonbills and egrets. Mugger crocodiles sun themselves on the banks.

name: Dubare Elephant Camp
teaser: Bathe and feed forest department elephants in the Cauvery river at dawn
about: Dubare Elephant Camp on the Cauvery River is run by the forest department. Visitors can interact with, feed and bathe captive-raised elephants. Best visited early morning during the bathing session.

name: Golden Temple, Bylakuppe
teaser: A Tibetan Buddhist monastery with a giant golden Buddha in rural Karnataka
about: The Namdroling Monastery (Golden Temple) in Bylakuppe is one of the largest Tibetan Buddhist monasteries outside Tibet. Built in 1963, it houses three 40-foot golden statues — Guru Padmasambhava, Buddha Sakyamuni and Amitayus.

name: Lalbagh Botanical Garden
teaser: Hyder Ali's 240-year-old botanical garden with a glass house modeled on London's Crystal Palace
about: Lalbagh is Bangalore's premier 240-acre botanical garden, built by Hyder Ali in 1760 and expanded by Tipu Sultan. Home to 1,800+ plant species, a glass house modeled on London's Crystal Palace, and a 3,000-million-year-old rock.

name: Cubbon Park
teaser: A 300-acre lung of Bangalore — heritage buildings hidden among old trees
about: Cubbon Park is a 300-acre public park in central Bangalore. Laid out in 1870, it contains heritage buildings including the High Court and a State Library, as well as old banyan trees and quiet walking paths.

name: Wonderla Amusement Park
teaser: South India's biggest theme park with 60+ rides and wave pools
about: Wonderla Bangalore is India's most visited amusement park. It has 60+ rides including roller coasters, water slides, wave pools and drop towers. Good for groups and families.

name: Skandagiri (Kalavara Durga)
best_time: Sep – Mar (night trek Dec – Feb is famous)
teaser: Bangalore's most famous night trek — summit above the clouds at sunrise
about: Skandagiri is Karnataka's most popular night trek starting point. The 8 km trail climbs through rocky scrub to a ruined fort at 1,450 m. The cloud inversion at dawn — seeing Nandi Hills above the clouds — is legendary.

name: Anthargange
teaser: Cave exploration and boulder scramble — an underground spring in volcanic rock
about: Anthargange (meaning 'underground Ganga') is a small hill with a cluster of volcanic rock formations, caves and an underground freshwater spring. Cave exploration with headlamps is the main activity, followed by a short trek to the hilltop temple.

name: Mysore Zoo
teaser: One of India's oldest and best zoos — white tigers and African animals in spacious enclosures
about: Sri Chamarajendra Zoological Gardens is one of India's best zoos. Founded in 1892, it houses over 1,500 animals across 168 species in well-maintained naturalistic enclosures. White tigers, African lions and gorillas are highlights.

name: Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary
teaser: Vast open plains where blackbuck herds and Indian wolves still roam
about: Ranibennur Blackbuck Sanctuary is a protected grassland sanctuary supporting large herds of blackbuck alongside wolves, jackals and foxes. The open savanna habitat is quite different from Karnataka's typical jungle parks.

name: Tungabhadra Dam (Hospet)
teaser: Musical fountain and sunset views over Tungabhadra — gateway to Hampi
about: Tungabhadra Dam is one of Karnataka's major dams on the Tungabhadra river. The evening musical fountain show is popular. It's commonly visited along with Hampi (12 km away).

name: Hogenakkal Falls
teaser: The Niagara of India — coracle rides through Kaveri gorge falls at the border
about: Hogenakkal (smoke that rocks) sits on the Kaveri river at the Karnataka–Tamil Nadu border. Multiple falls pour through a rocky gorge. Coracle rides take visitors directly under the falls. Oil bath massage is a local tradition.

details.fish_market: Fresh Kaveri fish sold at falls
details.note: Technically in Tamil Nadu but accessed from Karnataka

name: Biligiri Hills Trek
teaser: Temple peak trek through tiger reserve where Eastern and Western Ghats collide
about: The trek to Biligirirangana Swamy Temple atop BR Hills passes through dense forest. Tribal Soliga guides lead visitors through the wildlife reserve. The summit temple at 1,750 m has commanding views.

name: Cauvery Rafting (Bheemanakuppe)
teaser: Grade II–III whitewater rafting through forested Cauvery river gorge
about: The Cauvery river near Bheemanakuppe offers the closest whitewater rafting experience to Bangalore. A 14 km stretch through a narrow rocky gorge with Grade II and III rapids. Popular with corporate groups.

name: Savandurga
teaser: One of Asia's largest monolith hills — a bold granite dome above the plains
about: Savandurga is one of the largest monolithic rocks in Asia at 1,226 m. The two peaks — Karigudda (black hill) and Biligudda (white hill) — offer a challenging scramble up bare granite. Fort ruins at the top.

name: Mahmud Gawan Madrasa, Bidar
teaser: A 15th-century Persian madrasa with blue-glazed tile minarets — India's most overlooked ruin
about: Mahmud Gawan Madrasa (1472) was one of the finest educational institutions of the medieval Deccan. Three of its four minarets survive, decorated with intricate Persian blue-and-white calligraphy tiles — a rarity in India.

name: Nrityagram Dance Village
teaser: A gurukula dance village where classical odissi is kept alive in a forest setting
about: Nrityagram is a residential dance community and gurukula near Bangalore dedicated to classical Indian dance. Set in a forest, the community hosts weekend performances and open days. One of the rare living cultural institutions near the city.

name: Hampi Bouldering
teaser: World-class granite bouldering in the shadow of Vijayanagara ruins
about: Hampi's granite boulderscape is considered one of the world's top bouldering destinations. Hundreds of established problems across all grades sit amid ancient ruins and temples. The climbing and heritage combination makes it unique globally.

name: Nugu Bird Sanctuary
teaser: A quiet reservoir sanctuary rarely on the tourist map — migrant waders at their best
about: Nugu Wildlife Sanctuary and reservoir near H.D. Kote is one of Karnataka's lesser-known bird watching spots. The reservoir attracts migratory waders, ducks and wading birds during winter. Adjoins Nagarhole.

name: Male Mahadeshwara Hills
teaser: A forest pilgrimage to the shrine of male deity through MM Hills tiger corridor
about: Male Mahadeshwara (MM Hills) is a major pilgrimage site in a forest setting, dedicated to Lord Mahadeshwara. The winding road through the MM Hills Tiger Reserve offers wildlife sightings en route to the temple.

name: Raja's Seat, Madikeri
teaser: The king's garden terrace — where Coorg royalty watched the sun set over the Ghats
about: Raja's Seat (King's Seat) is a garden terrace in Madikeri from where Coorg kings watched sunsets over the Western Ghats. Today it's a well-maintained garden with a toy train and the most popular sunset viewpoint in Coorg.
